body_txt: A God Turned Mortal is a deity that has in some way, shape or form been transformed into a mortal, though to what extent varies. They might be 100% biologically mortal with no divine power whatsoever. Some might possess some of their powers, resulting in them effectively being either magic users or people with superpowers. Some examples might have a divine being downgraded to being a demigod. Other examples include Semi-Divine beings such as demigods, angels, or demons being reduced to mortals. Regardless, the former deity now lacks the complete benefits of godhood. The causes of this phenomenon can vary. Sometimes another deity might be the cause, with typical reasons including a punishment for a crime, an attempt to kill or weaken or rival, or as a result another deity stealing their powers. In settings where a god's powers are fueled by mortal worship , a deity might become mortal if they have too few worshippers to sustain their power. In settings in which gods can die and reincarnate, a dead god might be reborn as a mortal and may or may not have Past-Life Memories . In some cases, a god may even choose to voluntarily surrender their godhood for some reason, such as saving a loved one or a desire to see what it's like being mortal. Said former deity may or may not receive their godhood back by the end of the story. If a deity was turned into a mortal as a punishment, they might have their godhood returned if they atone for their crime. If a mortal is the reincarnation of a deity, they may find some way to access their former powers. In some cases, a deity might not get their godhood back either because they are content with being a mortal, they never atoned for whatever crime they did, or because there isn't any way for them to get back their godhood. Subtrope of De-power . Opposite Trope to Deity of Human Origin . Overlaps with Brought Down to Normal if a god only becomes a normal human or Brought Down to Badass if a god keeps some of their powers. See also Humanity Ensues which covers cases of non-humans becoming humans, though this trope also covers cases of gods turning into non-human beings such as animals, demihumans , and Talking Animals . Leads to Mortality Ensues in settings where gods are immortal. Can be a Karmic Transformation for deities that look down on and/or torment mortals. An Immortal Breaker can kill an immortal being. Not to be confused with God in Human Form , which is about deities being disguised as mortals but not necessarily losing their powers. Also this trope doesn't necessarily have to involve a deity changing form, just losing their godhood. Compare Amnesiac God , a story trope where a narrative follows a deity who has forgotten their nature and status; an Amnesiac God may or may lose their full powers and nature beyond losing knowledge of them.
